description: The Office of Congressman Robert Garcia (CA-42) is hiring for a Paid Part-Time Press Intern position as soon as possible to work in the Washington, D.C. office. The Press Intern works closely with the communications team. Base salary is $18/h. Tues-Thurs in person 9:00 a.m. - 2:00 p.m. and Mon & Fri remote 9:00 a.m. - 1:30 p.m. Flexible for the right candidate.
responsibilities: Drafting press releases and other written materials
Clipping committee remarks and cable hits
qualifications: Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should send a one page resume, 1 sample press release, and 3 relevant sample tweets to sara.guerrero@mail.house.gov, emily.kassner-marks@mail.house.gov, and ethan.cesar@mail.house.gov with "INTERN APP: Full Name, Spring 2025" in the subject line. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis.
equal_opportunity: All qualified applicants, including candidates of diverse backgrounds, are encouraged to apply. The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

position_title: Digital Media And Traditional Press Intern
office: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform Democrats
description: The House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform Democrats seek two motivated and reliable students to fill two full-time Digital Media and Traditional Press Intern positions this Summer. The intern responsibilities include, but are not limited to, written content creation, graphic design, video editing, and performing other press and administrative tasks as necessary. Successful candidates will possess familiarity with programs such as Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe InDesign, Canva, as well as compiling and interpreting social media analytics. Ability to work in a fast-paced, rapid response environment and willingness to learn are a must. This position is an integral part of the Communications Team and reports to the Communications Director. Candidates with diverse backgrounds and experiences are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Perform other press and administrative tasks as necessary
Compile and interpret social media analytics
qualifications: Familiarity with Adobe Premiere Pro, Adobe Photoshop, Adobe InDesign, and Canva
Ability to work in a fast-paced, rapid response environment
how_to_apply: To apply, please send a resume, cover letter, and portfolio to oversightpress@mail.house.gov.
